The gap arose because order state transitions were recorded via an asynchronous event-sourcing projection (ADR-014, designed ~September 2025), while the pre-existing domain rule required synchronous, immediately-durable commitment to a comp

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Preserving the Learning has 6 named artifacts (exceeds 3 minimum). ADR-015 owned by MJ covers the ADR update. Artifact 2 (regulated-domain-constraints.yaml) owned by Bird covers domain rule update. Artifact 3 (ADR review checklist) owned by Kobe covers the review checklist item. Tone is explicitly non-prosecutorial throughout, noting gaps were 'structural' not 'individual failures.'

# Learning Review: FCA Audit Trail Non-Compliance (2026-01-19)

## 1. Situation Snapshot

On 2026-01-19, an FCA audit flagged that 6 weeks of order state transition records for regulated instruments were non-compliant with COBS 11.2A audit trail requirements. The root issue was a semantic mismatch between MJ's event-sourced architecture (asynchronous projection to read models) and Bird's domain rule requiring synchronous, immediately-durable commitment to a compliance record store before confirm
